def: s.m. Shin-bone, shank; leg.
s.m. See s.v. nar, 'man.'
s.f. (dialec.) = nāṛī, and nālī, qq.v.
s.f. = next, q.v.
s.f. A stalk, or culm (of a plant); — any tubular organ of the body; an artery, a blood-vessel, vein; an intestine
subs: tube (i.q. nālī, or nalī); — a fistulous sore; a fistula; siuus; — the pulse (of the hand or foot, &c.); — an hour of twenty-four minutes (i.q. nāḍikā, q.v.)
s.m. The esculent root Arum colocasia
or nāṛī na bolnā, The pulse to stop beating; to have no pulse
or nāṛī-manḍal, s.m. The celestial equator
s.m. The planet of a person's nativity
s.m. A fistulous sore, a fistula, an ulcer, a sinus.

def: s.f. Dressed sheep-skin; kid (leather); roan; morocco.
s.f. A kind of fireworks (rockets without sticks, which run upon the ground).
adj. Fiery, full of fire; hellish.
s.f. A woman, a female; a wife
subs: adj. Devoted to women. — nārī-prasaṅg, s.m. Libertinism, lechery; coition
adj. & s.m. Loving women; — dear to women; — a lover of women; — one loved by women
s.m. (f. -ā), One who is a skilful judge of women
s.m. Woman's vice, any great fault in women (six are usually reckoned, viz. drinking spirits, keeping bad company, quitting a husband, rambling abroad, sleeping and dwelling in a strange house)
s.m. The duties especially incumbent on women; — the menstrual flux
or nāri-may, adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of women
or nāri-narā, s.m. Women and men; males and females
s.f. A clever woman; — a good housewife.

def: s.m. = ناڙيچ nāṛīć, q.v.
s.m. The esculent root Colocasia antiquorum.
s.f. = nāḍikā, q.v.
s.m. A cocoa-nut.
s.m. A cocoa-nut.
s.m. Lord of women, or of many wives'; an epithet of the moon.
(Braj or dialec.), s.f. pl. (of nārī), Women, females.
(Braj) s.f. pl. (of nāri or nārī), Women.

ety: nar, q.v.+īna = Zend aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Male; masculine.
s.m. (old H.) = narendra, q.v.s.v. nar, 'man.'

ety: nār = S. नाडि+iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. A person who feels the pulse.
s.m. A person who feels the pulse
subs: s.m. A physician skilled in feeling the pulse.

def: s.f. A tile.
s.m. The cocoa-nut tree, Cocos mucifera; — a cocoa-nut; — a ḥuqqa made of a cocoa-nut; — a kind of fire-work (made of a cocoa-nut shell charged with gunpowder); — (met.) the head, skull
subs: Milk of a cocoa-nut; — nāriyal-kā tel, Cocoa-nut oil
A kind of soft downy substance (of a light-brown colour) found on the exterior of the lower part of the leaves or branches of the cocoa-nut tree (it is used as a styptic, and for tinder)
Molasses made from the juice of the cocoa-nut palm.

ety: n āriyal, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A cup made out of a cocoa-nut shell; — a ḥuqqa made of a cocoa-nut shell; — (dialec.) juice of the cocoa-nut palm, palm-wine, toddy.
s.m. (dialec. or rustio) = next, q.v.

ety: prob. S. नाराच+कः; cf. S. नाराची or नाराचिका
def: s.m. A pair of small scales (such as is used by a goldsmith, &c.).
s.m. = نرجا narjā, q.v.
s.m. A cocoa-nut; — the cocoa-nut tree, Cocos mucifera.
s.m. A corr. Urdū form of نرکل narkal or narkul, q.v.

ety: Prk. णलकंडं; S. नड, or नल+काण्डं
def: s.m. (dialec.) A large species of reed (i.q. narsal, q.v.); a bulrush.
s.m. Trachea, windpipe.
s.m. = narkal, q.v.

ety: narkha˚, prob. = S. नालक+ṛā and ṛī = Prk. डओ and डिआ = S. र+कः and इका
def: The larynx, the throat, throttle, gullet.
s.m. = H نرخري narḵẖarī, s.f. dialec. forms of نرکهڙا narkhaṛā, &c., q.v.

ety: narkha˚, prob. = S. नालक+ṛā and ṛī = Prk. डओ and डिआ = S. र+कः and इका
def: The larynx, the throat, throttle, gullet.
s.f. = H نرخرا narḵẖarā, s.m. dialec. forms of نرکهڙا narkhaṛā, &c., q.v.
adj. Abounding in reeds (as a field, &c.).
s.m. (dialec.) Windpipe, &c. ( = naṛṛā, q.v.).
s.m. See s.v. nar, 'man.'

ety: Pehl. narm, or nam; Zend nãmya, rt. nam 'to bend'; S. नम्र, rt. नम्
def: adj. Soft (to the touch); smooth, sleek; ductile, plastic; — mild, tender, gentle, easy, yielding (as a disposition); simple, silly, soft; — soft, low, subdued (as a tone, &c.); — light, easy, moderate (as a price, &c.); — soft, easy of digestion; — slack, depressed, dull (as a market, or trade, &c.); — defective, inferior, old and defaced (as coin, &c.); — adv. Softly, gently, slowly, &c.
subs: s.m. Tender pasture or grass; — (fig.) soft articles of food; food cooked soft; food easy of digestion; spoon-diet

adj. & s.m. Neither very hot nor very cold, of moderate heat; — mild and sharp, easy (or mild) and rigorous, now forbearing now severe; — new and old; the best and the middling; good and bad; — the hot and the cold; the good and the bad; the vicissitudes (of life, or of fortune)
(-ke), To endure, or to put up with, the hot and the cold, or the good and the bad (of life, &c.)
adj. Exceedingly or very soft, &c.; — adv. Softly, gently.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَرْم (narm).

def: adj. & s.m. Soft (i.q. narm); — the soft; — the soft part (of)
subs: s.m. The soft part of the ear, the lobe of the ear.

ety: prob. for P. نرمه narma, rel. n. fr. narm
def: s.m. (dialec.) The silk-cotton plant, Gossypium religiosum.
s.m. See s.v. P. nar 'male.'
s.f. The river Narmada, or (modern) Nerbudda (which rises in the mountain Amar-kaṇṭak in the Vindhya range, and runs westward to the gulf of Cambay).

ety: narm, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) = نرمی narmī, q.v.

ety: narm, q.v.+dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: adj. Soft, smooth; delicate.

ety: narm, q.v. + ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To soften; to mollify, soothe, appease, pacify, mitigate, assuage; to subdue; — to moderate, reduce, lower, abate, lessen (as a price, &c.)
subs: or narmā-janā, v.n. To become soft, to soften; — to become gentle, or mild; to be subdued; — to relax, abate, lower, lessen.

def: s.f. (rustic or dialec.) Soft clayey soil.
s.m. See s.v. nar, 'man.'

s.f. Softness, smoothness, sleekness; — pliancy; — tenderness; delicacy; — gentleness, mildness; want of firmness; — tameness; — cringing, fawning, sycophancy (e.g. dushman-kī ćāplūsī o narmī-se mag̠rūr na ho); — remission, abatement, reduction; — easiness; fluency.

ety: narm, q.v.+A. يّة īyat, aff. of abst. s.
def: s.f. Softness, &c. (i.q. narmī).
adj. See s.v. nar, 'man.'
s.m. See s.v. nar, 'man.'
s.m. Men and women, males and females.
s.m. See s.v. nar, 'man.'

ety: Zend nairya, fr. nar; S. नर्य; — or rel. n. fr. nar, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Male; — manly; masculine; rough, rude; — ugly, abominable; filthy, sordid; — the penis; — trunk (of a tree); — ward (of a key); — a wave or billow; — a hermaphrodite; — a beggar.
s.m. (dialec.) The hard inner portion of a carrot.

ety: Prk. णाडिआ, or णालिआ; S. नाडिका, or नालिका
def: s.f. The windpipe, gullet, throat; — a weaver's shuttle, &c. (see nalī).
s.m. See s.v. nar, 'man.'
s.m. A large species of reed, Arundo karka (ḥuqqa-tubes are made of the stem, and mats with the leaves).

ety: Prk. णरसिंगओ or नरसिंगओ; S. नर+शृङ्गकः
def: s.m. A (musical) horn; a trumpet, or other similar wind-instrument; — (in contempt) a long pipe.

ety: Prk. णरसिंघो, or नरसिंघो; S. नर+सिंहः
def: The man-lion, Vishṇu in his fourth Avatār (see nar-siṅh, s.v. nar).
s.m. a corr. form of narsiṅgā, q.v.
s.m. One who plays on the horn; a trumpeter.
s.m. See s.v. nar, 'man.'

ety: Dravid. nal, 'four'+S. श्वस्; — or perhaps for ćarsoṅ = S. चतुर्+श्वस्
def: s.f. & adv. The fourth day past, or to come; four days ago, or four days hence; several days ago or hence.
s.m.f. See s.v. nar, 'man.'
s.m. & f. See s.v. nar, 'man.'
s.m. (f. -ī), Dancer; actor, mime, mummer, gesticulator; player (of any description); juggler; — a dancing-master.
s.f. A female dancer; an actress; a nāć-girl, singing-girl.
s.m. See s.v. nar, 'man.'
s.m. Dancing; acting; gesticulation; — (S. nartanah̥), s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), A dancer.
s.m. (f. -trī), 'One who causes to dance,' a dancing-master.
s.m. (f. -trī), 'One who causes to dance,' a dancing-master.
s.m. (dialec.) = nāhrū, or nahāruʼā, qq.v.

ety: prob. nar(a), q.v.+ukka = Mg. Prk. ऊके = S. क+कः
def: adj. (dialec.) Male; masculine.
adj. Abounding in reeds, reedy; — s.m. A quantity of reeds; a reed bed.
s.m. A species of lofty tree which gives much shade; the sour cherry; — the pomegranate tree (cf. nār).
s.m. A species of lofty tree which gives much shade; the sour cherry; — the pomegranate tree (cf. nār).
s.m. See s.v. nar, 'man.'
s.m. Destruction, &c. (i.q. nāś, q.v.)
subs: (-kā), To destroy, ruin, annihilate; to spoil, mar.

ety: Prk. णस्सं or नस्सं; S. नस्यं
def: s.f. Snuff
subs: s.m., or nās-dānī, s.f. A snuff-box

def: s.f. A vein; a muscle, sinew, tendon, nerve; fibre; — the organ of generation

s.m. A congeries of nerves; the nervous system.

def: s.m. Disappearance; loss perdition, destruction, ruin, devastation annihilation; non-existence; death
subs: v.t. To ruin, destroy, annihilate; to lose; to spoil, mar
v.n. To be or become lost, or ruined, or destroyed, &c.

def: 1˚, s.f. corr. of nas, 'vein,' q.v.; — 2˚, s.m. corr. of A. nashā, q.v.

r: nās, nāsnā, nās khonā, nās milānā
def: conj. part. of nāsnā, q.v.

s.f. The nose
subs: s.m. The wearing the nose-ring in the right nostril (the practice of rich women who have children)
s.m. Wearing the nose-ring in the left nostril (a mark of sorrow or distress)

def: s.m. The nose; — a tumour in the nose, a polypus (i.q. nakṛā, or nākṛā).
s.f. The nose.

def: s.m. Disappearance; destruction (i.q. nāś, q.v.).
s.m. corr. of نشا nashā, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نسا (nisā), from Arabic نِسَاء (nisāʔ).

s.m. (in Arith.) Denominator (of a fraction; — syn. bāṅṭ-baṭāʼū)
s.m. Genealogy and attainments or merits.

def: adj. Related (to), of the blood, lineal.
adj. (f. -ī), Having veins, or tendons, or muscles, &c.; veined, veiny, sinewy.
s.f. pl. (of naṣīḥat, q.v.), Counsels, advices, admonitions, &c.
s.f. pl. (of nasīm, q.v.), Breezes, gales.
adj. & s.m. = ناشك nāśak, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Causing to perish; removing, destroying, annihilating; destructive (of); — a destroyer, remover, dispeller; an antidote.

ety: v.n. fr. نسق 'to put in order,' &c.
def: s.m. Order; series; method, way, manner; — arrangement; management; — connection; — style, mode of writing.
s.m. A species of crow.
s.m. Arranger (of an army, &c.); — a chamberlain; an orderly officer; — an executioner
s.m. = ناشن nāśan, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Causing to be lost; causing to perish; destroying, removing, annihilating (i.q. nāśak); — a destroyer, &c.; — s.m. A destroying, destruction; removal; — ruin, perishing.
v.t. & n., corr. or dialec. form of nasānā, q.v.

ety: nasā˚ = Prk. नस्सावे(इ) or नस्साव(इ) = S. नश्य(ति)+caus. aug. आपय
def: v.t. 'To cause to be lost or to perish'; to destroy, efface, annihilate; to spoil; to squander; — v.n. To be lost; to be destroyed, be annihilated; to perish, to come to nought.
= A نصاري naṣārā, s.m.(?) Nazareth (the city, so called from its abounding in orchards); — (pl. of naṣrān), Nazarenes, Christians.
= P نصارا naṣārā, s.m.(?) Nazareth (the city, so called from its abounding in orchards); — (pl. of naṣrān), Nazarenes, Christians.
perf. part. of nasāwnā, q.v. (i.q. nasāya, p.p. of nasānā).
adj. & s.m. Destroying, &c.; — destructive.
v.t. & n. = nasānā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of نصب 'to erect, set up,' &c.
def: s.m. Erecting, setting up, establishing, fixing, planting, &c.; establishment, &c. (in Gram.) marking a consonant with the vowel fatḥa (-َ); the vowel fatḥa (in inflexion or declension)
subs: v.t. To erect, set up (at, -par), to establish, fix, plant, &c.
v.n. To be erected, or set up, &c.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Giving good advice, right-counselling; faithful (minister, &c.); — adviser, monitor, counsellor, sincere friend; faithful minister.

ety: for A. نصفة, fr. naṣf, 'reaching to the middle (of
def: s.f. Justice, equity; — an equitable transaction.
s.f. A bier (usually with a corpse); a coffin; — a litter (on which a sick person is carried); — a catafalque (on which a royal corpse is laid)
subs: or banāt-ě-naʻsh, s.f. 'The daughters of the Bier'; the constellation of the Bear (as well 'The Greater' as 'The Lesser'; they are both called the Bier; the three stars which go before the Bier being called banāt, and every individual star ibn naʻsh.

ety: for A. نشاة nashāt, and نَشًا nashaṉ, v.n. fr. نَشَا (for نشو
def: s.m. Intoxication (lit. & fig.), drunkenness; — headache or crop-sickness (from over-drinking); — intoxicating liquor or drug, an intoxicant; — (for A. nashaṉ) perception of odour; — an agreeable smell, fragrance
subs: or nashā-ḵẖẉur, vulg. nashā-ḵẖor, s.m. One who sots or topes, a drunkard; one who smokes or eats intoxicating drugs
or niśā-pānī, s.m. Intoxicating liquors or drugs; — a comprehensive or an indefinite term for intoxication
To drink intoxicating liquor, or bhang; — to take bribes
(-ko), Intoxicating drink, &c. to get to the head, to be or become intoxicated
(-ko), 'Wine, &c. to cloud the brain,' to be half seas over; — nashe-dār, adj. Possessing intoxicating properties, intoxicating
or nashe-kā ḵẖumār, s.m. The going off of intoxication, becoming sober; — the after-effects of intoxication, headache or crop-sickness from over-drinking

v.int. To cause or produce intoxication (to, or in), to intoxicate; — to be addicted to drinking intoxicating liquor or bhang, to sot, to tope; — to be in the habit of smoking or eating intoxicating drugs
or nashe-kī jhāṅj, s.f. The flush or excitement of wine or other intoxicant
or nashe-meṅ, adv. In a state of intoxication
or nashe-meṅ g̠arq-āb honā, To be completely besotted by drink, to be dead drunk
(-kā), The effects of drink to pass off, to recover from intoxication; to come to one's senses

def: s.m. = نشا nashā, q.v.
s.f. Intoxicating quality or property; intoxication.

ety: corr. of A. نشأة nashʼat, inf. n. of نشأ 'to grow,' &c.
def: s.f. Growing; being produced; springing up, appearing; — anything growing, or produced; — a product; a creation; — a creature.

ety: inf. n. of نشط 'to be lively or sprightly,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Loving pleasure or mirth, &c.; — one who is fond of sprightliness, or pleasure, &c.
(for A. nashʼatain, obl. dual of nashʼat), s.f. dual, 'The two creations,' this world and the next.

ety: prop. ناشئ nāshiʼ, act. part. of نشأ 'to grow,' &c.
def: part. adj. Growing; beginning; appearing; — apparent, evident; — s.m. & f. Who or what grows; — a boy or girl starting into youth.
(v.n. fr. نشد 'to know, recollect,' &c.), s.m.(?) Verses which people recite alternately.
adj. (f. -ī), Inebriant, intoxicating; — intoxicated; — sleepy-looking, drooping (eyes).

ety: lit. 'hungry, having an empty stomach; fasting'
def: s.m. Breakfast; repast, meal
subs: (-kā), To make a breakfast (of), to breakfast (on); to make a meal (of), to eat up, devour, swallow.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَاشْتَا (nāštā). First attested in c. 1591 as Middle Hindi ناشتا (naśta).

ety: lit. 'hungry, having an empty stomach; fasting'
def: s.m. Breakfast; repast, meal
subs: (-kā), To make a breakfast (of), to breakfast (on); to make a meal (of), to eat up, devour, swallow.

def: s.m. corr. of nāspāl, q.v.
s.f. A pear, Pyrus communis.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ناشپاتی (nāšpātī). Compare Hindi नाशपाती (nāśpātī), Punjabi ਨਾਸ਼ਪਾਤੀ (nāśpātī), Bengali নাশপাতি (naśpati), Uzbek nashvati, Uyghur نەشپۈت (neshpüt).

def: s.m. Spreading (a table or a carpet, &c.); expanding, extending, diffusing; publishing (news); — dispersing, scattering, strewing; — reviving, vivifying, restoring to life, raising from the dead; — rising from the dead, coming to life; — resuscitation; life
subs: (as ink on blotting-paper, or on bad paper).
wety: From Arabic نَشْر (našr)

def: part. (f. -ā), Lost; invisible, vanished; perished, destroyed, ruined, spoiled; annihilated; dissipated, wasted, squandered; — depraved
subs: s.m. Name of the fourth day in both halves of the month Bhādra
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Unconscious, insensible, fainted, swooned; — one who has lost consciousness or his senses
v.t. To destroy, ruin, spoil; to wipe out, annihilate; to waste, dissipate

def: s.f. A lost or abandoned woman, an adultress, a harlot.
s.f. = S نشٿتو नष्टत्व nashṭatva, s.m. Destruction, ruin; annihilation; waste, dissipation; depravity.
s.m. = S نشٿتا नष्टता nashṭatā, s.f. Destruction, ruin; annihilation; waste, dissipation; depravity.
s.f. Loss, destruction, ruin.

ety: for A. نشوة nashwat, inf. n. of نَشَا (rt. نشو
def: s.m. Intoxication, drunkenness; exhilaration (from wine, &c.), hilarity.
adj. & s.m. = ناشي nāśī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Destroying; destructive; removing; — perishing; perishable; — a destroyer; remover, dispeller, &c.
s.f. A coulter, ploughshare.
part. adj. & s.m. Erecting, setting up, fixing, planting, placing; constituting; — one who erects, &c.
s.m. Part, portion; chance, lot; luck, good fortune; — destiny (in Urdū this word is generally constructed as a plural)
subs: (-ke, mere, &c.), To be unlucky, or unfortunate
naṣīb khulnā (-ke, mere, tere), One's star to be in the ascendant; to be fortunate, to be prosperous
v.t. To make (a thing) the lot or portion (of, -ko), to allot, apportion
(laṛne), Fortune to fight for or to favour (a person), to begin to be prosperous (after adversity); — to try (one's) luck, to be a competitor (for)
(-ke, mere, &c.), To fall to the lot (of), to have the good fortune (to gain anything); to be destined (for)
To have no luck; to be unfortunate; to be frustrated, to be deprived (of, -se).

ety: for A. مصيبة naṣībat, fem. of naṣīb
def: s.m. Lot, luck, fortune (i.q. naṣīb)
adj. Of fortune, or luck, or destiny; appointed, allotted (by destiny).

ety: nasī, q.v.+S. कः, with euphonic h inserted
def: s.m. A light plough (for soft soils).

ety: for A. نصيحة, v.n. fr. نصح 'to advise,' &c.
def: s.f. Advice, counsel, admonition, exhortation, precept; — reproof, reprimand; — chastisement

or naṣīḥat karnā, v.t. To advise, counsel, admonish, exhort; — to reprove, reprimand; to chastise
or naṣīḥat-go, s.m. Adviser, counsellor, admonisher.

ety: act. part. of نسج 'to weave; to compose,' &c.
def: s.m. A weaver; — a composer; maker of an oration.

ety: act. part. of نسك 'to be religious, or devout,' &c.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Transcribing, copying; — abolishing, obliterating, erasing; — copier (of a book); amanuensis; — one who abolishes, or obliterates, or erases, or cancels.

ety: nas, q.v. + Prk. अइल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. Sinewy; veined, veiny.
adj. Sinewy; veiny; — (formed after the model of the A., fr. nast), of good breed.

ety: inf. n. of نسم 'to breathe gently' (a wind, or an odour
def: s.f. A gentle breeze, zephyr, fragrant air, spicy gale

ety: act. part. of نثر 'to scatter, disperse,' &c.
def: s.m. A prose-writer.
s.m. Defender, assistant, helper, deliverer, ally.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic نَاصِر (nāṣir).

def: s.m. Succourer, assistant, helper, ally, defender, friend (i.q. nāṣir).
part. (f. -ā), Lost; suffered to perish; destroyed, ruined, annihilated.
adj. Ill-omened (cf. nisaṅṭh).
s.m. Forelock over the forehead; — the forehead (i.q. jabīn; peshānī).

ety: for A. نسيئة nasīʼat, fr. nasī, rt. نسأ 'to delay; to give credit,' &c.
def: s.m. Delay, adjournment, respite; — delay allowed for payment of money; credit, purchase or sale on credit.
s.m. Weaving (cloth).

ety: inf. n. of نسخ 'to transcribe, to copy'; — ʻto cancel, abrogate,' &c.
def: s.m. Transcribing, copying (a book, &c.); — cancelling, abrogating, repealing, &c.; cancelment, abolition, abrogation, repeal, annulment; — transformation (esp. into an ugly shape); — the modern Arabic character
subs: v.t. To transcribe, copy; — to cancel, abrogate, abolish, repeal, annul; to reject, to set aside; to confute.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَسْخ (nasx), borrowed from Arabic نَسْخ (nasḵ).

ety: v.n. fr. نسل 'to beget, or bring forth'
def: s.f. Offspring, progeny, lineage, pedigree, genealogy, descendants; descent, relationship, stock, race, breed, caste, family
wety: Borrowed from Arabic نَسْل (nasl).

def: adv. By descent
subs: adv. In regular descent, or succession

def: adj. Of or relating to family; genealogical; — of family.
adj. See s.v. nāś.

ety: nās˚ = Prk. नस्स(इ) = S. नश्य (ति), rt. नश्
def: v.n. To be lost, to disappear, vanish; to be destroyed; to perish; to wane; — to escape, flee, run away.

ety: nās˚ = Prk. नास(इ) or नासे(इ) = S. नाशय(ति), caus. of rt. नश्
def: v.t. To cause to disappear; to cause to perish; to destroy, ruin, efface, exterminate; — to lose.

ety: nas˚ = Prk. नस्स(इ) = S. नश्य(ति), rt. नश्
def: v.n. (dialec.) To disappear, vanish, make off, escape, run away, flee.

ety: v.n. fr. quadr. v. نَسْنَسَ 'to move or fly rapidly'
def: s.m. A kind of ape, a marmoset; an ourang-outang; a satyr, fawn; a monstrous race of men or demons, who have only one leg and one arm, and move by leaping.
adj. Of or relating to the nasnās; monstrous.
s.m. The rind of a pomegranate (ripe or unripe).
adj. Of or relating to, or obtained from, pomegranate-rind; of the colour of pomegranate-rind
subs: s.m. A kind of light-green colour extracted from pomegranate-rind.

def: s.f. corr. of nāshpātī, q.v.
s.f. A prose composition; prose.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic نَثْر (naṯr).

def: s.m. A vulture; — an eagle
subs: or nasr-ě-t̤āʼir, s.m. 'The flying eagle,' the constellation of the Eagle
s.m. 'The falling vulture,' the constellation of the Lyre.

ety: inf. n. of نصر 'to succour, help,' &c.
def: s.m. Succour, assistance, aid; — victory.
s.m. The constellations of the Eagle and the Lyre.

ety: rel. n. fr. naṣrān, 'a Nazarene, a Christian'
def: adj. Belonging to Christians or to Christianity; Christian; — s.m. A Christian (but the word is not used by Christians).
wety: Ultimately from Arabic نَصْرَانِيّ (naṣrāniyy).

def: s.f. The Christian religion, Christianity.
s.m. The Dog-rose, or Eglantine (i.q. nastaran).

ety: inf. n. of نصّ 'to elevate; to make manifest,' &c.
def: s.m. Showing, manifesting, exhibiting, demonstrating, setting forth; — demonstration, proof; — a text (of the Qorʼān), an explicit and decisive dictum (of law, or scripture).
s.m. A Weaver.
s.m. A writer of prose, or a good prose-writer.
adv. It is not; no, not so (cf. P. nest or nīst); — s.f. Non-existence, non-entity, annihilation; — adj. Rendered extinct, annihilated.
s.f. A hole bored in the septum of the nose (cf. nāth, and nath).
s.m. The nose; — (nastam) a sternutatory, snuff.
s.m. A hole bored in the septum or bridge of the nose of draught-cattle.
s.m. A kind of Persian writing (or character), a fine round hand.
s.m. The dog-rose, or eglantine (i.q. nasrīn); — the China rose, the white rose (of India), Rosa glandulifera; — a kind of cloth.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), 'Saying (or one who says) nāsti, "it is notʼ"; denying, atheistical, unbelieving; — one who denies the authority of the Vedas, or who expresses doubts as to the authenticity of the Purāṇas; — one who denies a future state; — an atheist, unbeliever, infidel
s.f. = S ناستکتو नास्तिकत्व nāstikatva, s.m. Heterodoxy (esp in reference to the authority of the Vedas, &c.); atheism, unbelief, impiety.
s.m. = S ناستکتا नास्तिकता nāstikatā, s.f. Heterodoxy (esp in reference to the authority of the Vedas, &c.); atheism, unbelief, impiety.
s.m. = nāstikatva, q.v.
s.f. = S ناستتو नास्तित्व nāstitva, s.m. Non-existence (i.q nāsti or nāst).
s.m. = S ناستتا नास्तिता nāstitā, s.f. Non-existence (i.q nāsti or nāst).
s.m. Wounding — a running sore, an ulcer; a fistula
subs: (-meṅ), An ulcer, &c. to form; to become a running sore, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic نَاسُور (nāsūr), from Classical Syriac ܢܳܨܘܿܪܴܐ (nāṣōrā, “sore, ulcer”).

ety: nās, q.v.+ūt, a termination borrowed from the Aramaic
def: s.f. Humanity, human nature; human kind.
adj. corr. of نشور naśvar, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā, or -ī), Perishing, perishable; destructible; liable to decay, transitory, evanescent; — destructive, mischievous.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نُشُور (nušūr), from Arabic نُشُور (nušūr).

def: s.f. = S نشورتو नश्वरत्व naśvaratva, s.m. Perishableness; transitoriness, evanescence; destructiveness, mischievousness.
s.m. = S نشورتا नश्वरता naśvaratā, s.f. Perishableness; transitoriness, evanescence; destructiveness, mischievousness.
adj. Of the nose, belonging or rela ting to the nose; — s.m. A nose-string; rein (of a draught-ox) passed through the septum of the nostrils.
s.f. The nose; — nose-string (of an ox, or camel, &c.; syn. nāth).
adj. (f. -ā), Belonging or relating to the nose; nasal; — (S. nasyam), s.m. A sternutatory, snuff.
adj. Forgetful oblivious.
part. Perishing, decaying; being destroyed; — waning; wasting.
part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Fastened by the nose; led by a string through the septum of the nose; — an ox, or other animal, led by a string through the septum of the nose.
s.m. 1˚ = nātā, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of nāth, q.v.
s.f. (colloq. or corr.) = نتهہ nath, q.v.

ety: S. नटः, rt. नट्, corr. fr. नृत्
def: s.m. (f. -ī, -in, -nī, -inī), A dancer; rope-dancer; tumbler; mime, actor; juggler; — name of a particular vagrant caste, the Indian gipsy (said to be the offspring of a degraded Kshatriya by a woman of the second caste; they are generally rope-dancers, jugglers, &c.); — name of a subordinate mode in music; — the tree Calosanthes indica; the tree Jonesia asoka; — a sort of reed, Arunda tibialis, or A. karka
subs: s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode (according to some, the sixth, or, according to others, a subdivision of that called dīpak, or of megh)
vulg. naṭ-bidyā, s.f. The art of rope-dancing, or tumbling, &c.

def: s.f. A description; an epithet; a descriptive epithet or phrase, a noun-adjective; — praise, eulogium, encomium (esp. of the prophet Moḥammad).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَعْت (na't), from Arabic نَعْت (naʕt).

def: s.f. (dialec.) A pillar; an obelisk; — an architrave; — a beam; a log (of wood).
s.m. Relative; kin; relationship; kinsmanship; alliance; affinity, consanguinity
subs: (se, or -ke sāth), To form a connection, or an alliance (with)

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Short, dwarfish; dapper; — vile, depraved, vicious, bad, naughty; — a dwarf; — a depraved or bad man; a naughty boy; — a young bull not yet broken in.
s.f. A species of shrub, Caesalpina bonducella.
s.m. Dancing; a dance; a play, a pantomime; — the country called the Carnatic
subs: s.m. Dancing-saloon, ball-room; a room or hall for dramatic performances, a theatre.

def: part. (f. -a), Bent, bowed; inclined, inclining; crooked, curved; depressed, sunk
subs: (˚ta+an˚), adj. (f. -ī), Bowing the limbs; bending the body; bowing, bent, stooping; curved
or nat, s.m. (dialec.) Any turbinate univalve shell (with, or without, the creature in it); a snail; periwinkle, cockle, whelk, mussel, &c.
adj. & adv. & s.m. (f. -ī), Bending down the face, looking down
with the face down; — a person who looks down
or who has his face down.

def: s.m. Zenith-distance at meridian transit (of a planet); hour-angle, or the distance (of a planet) from the meridian
s.m. pl. (of natīja, q.v.), Fruits, consequences, issues, &c.

ety: nātā, q.v.+āʼit = Prk. आइत्तो = आयंतो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन् (the aff. of the pres. part. with the caus. aug. āpi)
def: adj. & s.m. Related, kin; — a relation, relative, kinsman.

s.m. (f. -ikā, or -ī), A dancer; an actor; a mime; — dancing; acting; — a play, drama, comedy; the first of the ten species of Sanskrit dramatic compositions of the first order; — the business of a naṭ (q.v.)
subs: vulg. nāṭak-sālā, or nāṭak-sāl, s.f. A dancing-room, ball-room; a play-house, theatre (syn. nāć-ghar)
(prob. for nāṭak-sālī), s.m. & f. (Dakh.) A dancer, &c. (i.q. nāṭak).

def: s.f. A dancing-girl; — a kind of dramatic writing.
adj. Dramatic; comic.
m. The beginning of a particular holy text.
s.m. (Dakh.) A great merchant; a respectable man; head-man (syn. mahājan).
s.f. Denial; negation; refusal.
adj. See s.v. nata or nat.
s.m. See s.v. nat.

ety: nāṭā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Shortness (of stature), dwarfishness.
conj. (dialec.) If not — then; else, otherwise (i.q. nā-to, or nā-tau, or nahīṅ to).
conj. (dialec.) = nātar or nātari, q.v.
s.f. The state or condition, or the office, of an actor, &c.; actorship.
s.m. = naṭ-bar, q.v.; — 2˚ adj. corr. of nā-ṭhaur, q.v.
s.m. Chief dancer or actor; — dancer, rope-dancer, &c. (i.q. naṭ, q.v.).
v.t. To turn up (the eyes), to close (the eyes, in death).

ety: Prk. नत्था; S. नस्ता; — cf. also S. नाथः, which is prob. a Prk. form
def: s.f. The nose-string of an ox (by which he is led, or which serves as reins); — a seton (cf. nāth, nathnā, nathnī)
subs: na āge nāth na pīćhe paghā
'Neither nose-string in front nor tether behind'; free to roam; without tie or encumbrance.

def: s.m. (f. -ā), Protector, patrou, master, lord; husband; — a common affix to the names of Hindū ascetics (e.g. Gorakh-nāth)
subs: nāth-vat, or nāth-want (f. -vatī), adj. Having a protector, or master; having a husband; dependent, subject (to; — syn. nāthī).

def: s.m. Non-existence.

s.f. (or m.?) A rope passed through the nose of a draught-ox, nose-string (i.q. nāth, q.v.); — a large (gold or silver) ring worn (by women) in the nose (on the left nostril); nose-ring (widows are not allowed to wear either Lose-rings or bangles; hence, the benediction, ilāhī, terī nath-ćūṛī bar-qarār raheṅ)
subs: (apnī), To take off her nose-ring (a woman).

def: s.m. (dialec.) A corrupt form of nātā, q.v.
(Braj), s.f. pl. of nath, q.v.
s.f. = S ناتهتو नाथत्व nāthatva, s.m. The office or status of a protector, or master; protectorship; patronage, &c
s.m. = S ناتهتا नाथता nāthatā, s.f. The office or status of a protector, or master; protectorship; patronage, &c
adj. Having no fixed or proper place; not in (its) proper place, out of place (i.q. be-ṭhaur).
part. (of nāthnā), Having put a string in the nose (of an ox), i.q. nāth-ke.
adj. (f. -inī), Having a protector, or master; having a husband; dependent, subject (to).
adj. Having a hole bored in the septum of the nose, or having a string through its nose (an ox or other draught-animal).
s.m. (rustic) A vagabond; scoundrel; a term of abuse.
s.f. (dialec.) dim. of nath, and = nath or nathnī, qq.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = nathnī, q.v.

ety: fr. nāth, q.v.; or nāth˚ = S. नस्तित
def: v.t. To bore the nose (of a bullock) and put a string in it (to guide him by); — to bring under subjection or control.

ety: nath, q.v.+nā = lā = Prk. अलओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. A nostril; — a large ring for the nose
subs: (with gen.), To pierce (one's) nostrils with an arrow
(mere, or us-ke), 'The breath to come into (one's) nostrils,' to be worried, or plagued, or harassed
or nathne ćaṛhānā, To distend, or to turn up, the nostrils; to be enraged, or displeased
(merā or kisī-kā) nathnoṅ-tak ā-jānā, 'The soul to come as far as the nostrils'; to be ready to drop, or faint, or die (from exhaustion, or worry, &c.); to be worried to death, to be sorely tried.

def: v.n. To have the nose bored, and a string passed through it (a bullock).

ety: nath, q.v.+nī = lī = Prk. अलिआ = S. ल+इका
def: s.f. A nose-ring (i.q. nath, q.v.); — a small ring inserted in the hilt of a sword; — a string passed through (or tied round) a bullock's nose (by which to guide him)
subs: (-kī), To be dishonoured; to be violated (a virgin).

def: s.m. (f. nātin), Daughter's son, grandson.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ناتی (naty /⁠nātī⁠/), from Old Hindi नांती (nāṃtī /⁠nā̃tī⁠/), from Apabhramsa णत्तिअ (ṇattia), from Prakrit 𑀡𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀅 (ṇattia), from Sanskrit नप्तृ (naptṛ) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.f. (dialec.) Granddaughter (i.q. nātin).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ناتی (naty /⁠nātī⁠/), from Old Hindi नांती (nāṃtī /⁠nā̃tī⁠/), from Apabhramsa णत्तिअ (ṇattia), from Prakrit 𑀡𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀅 (ṇattia), from Sanskrit नप्तृ (naptṛ) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.f. Bending, bowing, stooping; curvature, crookedness; — a bow; a courtesy; reverential salutation or address (i.q. namaskār); modest behaviour.
s.f. An actress, a dancer, a nāć-girl; a harlot, courtezan; — a species of fragrant plant (used in medicine); — red arsenic.

ety: for A. نتيجة natījat, v.n. fr. نتج 'to bring 'forth'
def: s.m. Fœtus; offspring; birth; — issue, fruit; consequence, result, effect, what necessarily follows, inference, conclusion (of a syllogism, &c.); moral (of a story, &c.); corollary; — sum, or substance; — retribution, reward
subs: (-se), To draw an inference (from), to infer, to conclude
(-kā), To be the issue, or fruit, or outcome (of); to be the result or consequence (of); — to ensue (from), to follow.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Speaking; — rational (animal); — perspicuous (book, &c.); — imperative (order, or decree); positive, decisive, definitive, final, conclusive; — a speaker; — a rational animal.
s.f. A short or light comedy (the first of the dramas of the second order); — (fem. of nāṭaka), an actress.

ety: for A. ناطقة nāt̤iqat, fem. of nāt̤iq
def: adj. Speaking, &c. (i.q. nāt̤iq, q.v.); — s.m. The faculty of speech (i.q. quwwat-ě-nāt̤iqa).
s.f. = naṭnī, q.v.

ety: f. of nātī; — ˚in or ˚inī = Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. Daughters's daughter, granddaughter.
wety: From ناتی (nātī, “grandson”) + ـاِن (-in).

def: s.f. = nātin, q.v.

ety: f. of nātī; — ˚in or ˚inī = Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. Daughters's daughter, granddaughter.

ety: dim. of nāṭā, q.v.; — ˚iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. (rustic or dialec.) A short or diminutive man; — a bullock of an inferior stock.

ety: prob. S. नट+शठः, corr. to षठ
def: adj. & s.m. Naughty, wicked, mischievous; — roguish, waggish; shrewd, artful, trickish; — a naughty or mischievous child, an imp; — a rogue; a trickster, cheat.

ety: the preceding + ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Naughtiness, wickedness, mischievousness, mischief; roguishness, artfulness, trickery

ety: S. nata, q.v.+Prk. अलो, and अलिआ = S. ल, and ल+इका
def: adj. & s.f. (dialec.) Curved, crooked, winding, spiral, turbinate; — a snail; a cockle, &c. (i.q. nat-gullā, q.v.s.v. nata).
v.n. = naṭnā, q.v.

ety: naṭ˚ = nāṭ˚ = S. नास्ति 'it is not'+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v. int. To say 'no' (with respect to, -se), to deny, disallow, disclaim, disown; to decline, refuse.

ety: naṭ˚ = Prk. नट्ट(इ) or नट्टे(इ) = S. नर्तय(ति), fr. नर्त
def: v.n. To dance; — to strut, or mince; — to act or perform; to assume a dramatic dress or character.
s.f. (colloq. or dialec.) = نتهني nathnī, q.v.
s.f. The wife of a naṭ; a woman of the naṭ tribe; — a female rope-dancer, &c. (see naṭ); an actress; a nāć-girl.
s.m. = nātā, q.v.; — 2˚ conj See s.v. nā.
part. adj. (dialec.) = nathrā, q.v.

ety: S. nata, q.v.+Prk. अलो, and अलिआ = S. ल, and ल+इका
def: adj. & s.f. (dialec.) Curved, crooked, winding, spiral, turbinate; — a snail; a cockle, &c. (i.q. nat-gullā, q.v.s.v. nata).
part. adj. (old H.) Destroyed, &c. (see nashṭ).

ety: natth or nath, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. The thread or string with which a file of papers are strung together; — a record, or file (of papers) threaded together; the record or papers of a cause or suit
subs: v.t. To string (papers) together, to file.

def: conj. (dialec.) = nā-tau, or nahīṅ-to, qq.v.
s.m. (rustic) A rope-dancer, &c. (i.q. naṭ, q.v.); — a dancing boy.
s.m. The science or art of dancing, or acting; scenic art; the union of song, pantomime, dance, and instrumental music

ety: Prk. एहाविओ, or नाविओ; S नापितः
def: s.m. (dialec.) A barber (i.q. nāʼī, q.v.).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀸𑀯𑀸 (ṇāvā), from Sanskrit नौ (náu), नावा (nāvā), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *náHuṣ, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *náHuš, from Proto-Indo-European *néh₂us. Cognate with Hindi नाव (nāv), Pali nāvā, and Punjabi ਨਾਉ (nāu). Cognate through Indo-European with Ancient Greek ναῦς (naûs), Persian ناو (nâv), and Spanish nave.

def: s.f. A boat (i.q. nāʼo, the pop. form, q.v.)
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi नौ (nau), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀯 (ṇava), from Sanskrit नव (náva).

def: s.m. Species (opp. of jins, 'genus'), kind, sort; manner, mode
subs: adv. At any rate, anyhow, at all events.

def: adj. Nine
subs: (˚va+aṉ˚), s.m. A ninth part, a ninth
s.m. Name of one of the peaks of the Himālaya
(nava-bhakti), s.f. The nine kinds of devotion recognized by Hindūs

or nau-duʼār (nava-dvār), adj. & s.m. Having nine doors or gates, nine-doored; — an epithet of the body (which has nine apertures or outlets, viz. the nostrils, the eyes, the ears, the mouth, the penis, and the anus); — the nine doors or outlets (of the body)
s.m. nau-rātri, s.f. or nau-rātrik, s.m. (nava-rātra, &c.), The period of nine days (from the first of the light half of the month Aśvin to the ninth) devoted to the worship of Durgā

or nau-ratan (nava-ratna), s.m. The nine precious gems (viz. a pearl, ruby, topaz, diamond, emerald, lapis lazuli, coral, sapphire, and one called Go-meda; — these nine jewels are supposed to be related to the nine planets); — a bracelet consisting of nine jewels; — the nine jewels, i.e. men of letters, at the court of Vikramāditya (viz. Dhanvantari, Kshapaṇak, Amarsingh, Śaṇku, Vetāl-bhaṭṭ, Ghaṭakarpar, Kālidās, Vararući, and Varāha-mihir); — a council of nine wise men
s.m. The aggregate of the nine kinds of pleasure (ras) recognized by the Hindu system
s.m. 'Nine and seven'; a division of the crop whereby the zamīndār takes nine parts out of sixteen, and the cultivator the remaining seven
s.m. 'Nine and seven'; the aggregate of the sixteen kinds of siṅgār (q.v.)
nau-śāyak (nava-śāyak), s.m. The designation applied to any of the nine inferior classes (viz. cow-herd, gardener, oilman, weaver, confectioner, water-carrier, potter, blacksmith, and barber)
or nau-srāddh, s.m. The first of the series of sacrifices to the manes of a deceased relative (viz. on the first, third, fifth, seventh, ninth, and eleventh days after the death)
s.m. The nine divisions or climes of the earth which constitute Jambu-dvīp, the central portion of the world, or the known world
or nau-girah, or nau-grahā (nava-grah, &c.), s.m. The nine planets (i.e. seven planets and the ascending and descending nodes)
adj. (f. -ī), Worth, or possessing, nine lacs (lākh)
s.m. The feast given in the ninth month of pregnancy
s.m. The period of nine months
or nau-nidh (nava-nidhi), s.m. The treasure of Kuver, which consists of nine fabulous gems
To be in possession of all the heart can desire
s.m. An ornament worn on the upper arm, consisting of nine precious stones set in gold or silver.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi नौ (nau), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀯 (ṇava), from Sanskrit नव (náva).

def: s.m. (dialec.) A barber (i.q. nāʼū; cf. nāʼī).

ety: for A. نوبة, v.n. of un. of ناب (for نوب
def: s.f. A period, time, turn; — access (of fever, &c.); paroxysm; fit; — occasion, opportunity; — vicissitude; — degree, pitch; weight, pass; — state (of matters or things); plight; time for recourse, or resort (to); — accident, misfortune, calamity; — relieving guard; keeping watch; — musical instruments, or drums, sounding at the gate of a great man at certain intervals; — a large kettle-drum (syn. naqqāra)
subs: or naubat jhaṛnā, The naubat to be sounded; drums, or the kettle-drum. to be beaten (at stated hours)
(-kī), Things or matters to come to such a pass or pitch; occasion for (certain things) to arrive or arise; recourse or resort was had to (e.g. laṭh-talwār-kī naubat pahuṅćī)
(kisī-kī) pahuṅćnā (yahāṅ-tak or lag), The case or state (of a person) to come (to such a pass)
s.m. A guard-house; a large room over the outer gate of a palace or a shrine where the drums or other musical instruments are sounded
s.f. The request or application for a turn (or hearing) in a court of law
naubat honā (-kī), To reach a (certain) point, to come to (such) a pass.
ex: ہماری جیت کی نوبت اب آ گئی ہے۔
hamārī jīt kī naubat ab ā gaī hai.
The conditions favourable for our victory have arrived.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نوبت (nawbat), from Arabic نَوْبَة (nawba).

def: adj. Periodical; intermittent (as a fever); — s.m. The man who beats the naubat or kettle-drum.
s.m. = nićhāwar, q.v.

ety: from the P. nauća, 'a young man,' fr. nau, 'young'
def: s.f. A young girl kept by a bawd; a harlot.

ety: Prk. नवधअं; S. नव+ध (rt. धा) + कं
def: adj. & s.m. Newly-planted (garden, &c.); — a newly-planted garden, or grove, &c.; — a sapling, a young plant; a fresh shoot or branch (of a plant or tree).

ety: Prk. नवधअं; S. नव+ध (rt. धा) + कं
def: adj. & s.m. Newly-planted (garden, &c.); — a newly-planted garden, or grove, &c.; — a sapling, a young plant; a fresh shoot or branch (of a plant or tree).
s.f. = next, q.v.
s.f. A bracelet consisting of nine precious stones set in gold or silver (cf. nau-ratn).

ety: for A. نوحة nauḥat, fr. nauḥ, inf. n. of ناح (for نوح
def: s.m. Lamentation; moaning, moan; wail (over the dead)
subs: s.m. One who laments, a mourner; — a hired mourner

def: v.n. (dialec.) = nauṛhnā, q.v.
adj. Constituting a species; of or relating to a species, specific.
s.f. Specific difference; — specification, special character, speciality.
